assertions.expectation: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Pod Disruption Budgets (Most Common)' and identifies PDBs as the most likely culprit in the immediate action plan

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
assertions.reason: Includes 'kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces' and 'kubectl describe pdb --all-namespaces' commands

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
assertions.reason: Multiple commands provided: 'kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o wide', 'kubectl get pods --all-namespaces --field-selector=spec.nodeName=<stuck-node-name>', and commands to find pending pods

assertions.expectation: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
assertions.reason: Includes commands to check for pending pods with 'kubectl get pods --all-namespaces --field-selector=status.phase=Pending'

assertions.expectation: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
assertions.reason: Multiple PDB fixes provided: 'kubectl patch pdb' commands to adjust minAvailable and maxUnavailable settings

assertions.expectation: Mentions that GKE has a PDB timeout (up to 1 hour) after which it may force-drain, and that GKE sends notifications when eviction is blocked by PDB
assertions.reason: Response does not mention GKE's PDB timeout behavior or notification system for blocked evictions

assertions.expectation: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
assertions.reason: Provides commands to configure max-surge and max-unavailable settings in multiple places, including prevention section

assertions.expectation: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
assertions.reason: Response does not mention admission controller webhooks or webhook issues as a potential cause of stuck drains

assertions.expectation: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
assertions.reason: Section 6 'Monitor Progress' includes 'watch kubectl get nodes' and 'kubectl get events' commands to monitor upgrade progress
